thm:3.5.3.summary: Perfectoid correspondence for analytic fields: an equivalence between perfectoid analytic fields $F$ and pairs $(L, I)$ where $L$ is a perfect analytic field of characteristic $p$ and $I \subset W(\mathfrak{o}_L)$ is principal generated by an element primitive of degree $1$. The Kedlaya–Liu form of tilting (parallel to Scholze thm:3.7), formulated explicitly via Witt vectors and primitive ideals.

thm:3.5.6.summary: For $F$ and $(L,I)$ corresponding via thm:3.5.3, the finite-étale categories satisfy a tensor equivalence $\mathbf{F\acute{E}t}(F) \cong \mathbf{F\acute{E}t}(L)$; every finite extension of $F$ is perfectoid, and the absolute Galois groups of $F$ and $L$ are homeomorphic. Parallel to Scholze thm:1.3.

thm:3.5.11.summary: Any perfectoid analytic field is deeply ramified. Connects perfectoid theory to Gabber–Ramero almost mathematics.

thm:3.6.5.summary: Perfectoid correspondence for adic Banach algebras: equivalence between perfectoid uniform Banach $\mathbb{Q}_p$-algebras $A$ and pairs $(R, I)$ where $R$ is a perfect uniform Banach $\mathbb{F}_p$-algebra and $I \subset W(\mathfrak{o}_R)$ is principal of primitive-degree-$1$. The Kedlaya–Liu version of the tilting equivalence; parallel to Scholze thm:1.7 / thm:5.2.

thm:3.6.14.summary: For $(A,A^+) \leftrightarrow ((R,R^+), I)$ corresponding as in thm:3.6.5, the homeomorphism $\mathcal{M}(A) \cong \mathcal{M}(R)$ lifts to a functorial homeomorphism $\mathrm{Spa}(A,A^+) \cong \mathrm{Spa}(R,R^+)$ identifying rational subspaces. Parallel to Scholze thm:1.8 / thm:6.3.

thm:3.6.15.summary: Any perfectoid adic Banach algebra is stably uniform and sheafy, satisfying Tate sheaf and Kiehl glueing. The Kedlaya–Liu route to the sheaf property — uses uniformity to bypass almost-mathematics machinery.

thm:3.6.17.summary: Density and perfectoid stability under bounded homomorphisms with dense image. Lets perfectoid structure be checked on dense subalgebras.

thm:3.6.21.summary: For $A \leftrightarrow (R,I)$ as in thm:3.6.5: any $B \in \mathbf{F\acute{E}t}(A)$ is perfectoid uniform Banach, and tilting induces a tensor equivalence $\mathbf{F\acute{E}t}(A) \cong \mathbf{F\acute{E}t}(R)$. Algebra-level analog of thm:3.5.6 and parallel to Scholze thm:5.25.

thm:3.7.4.summary: Preperfectoid algebras are stable under rational localization, hence stably uniform, sheafy, and satisfy Tate sheaf + Kiehl glueing. The Kedlaya–Liu workhorse for promoting algebras to perfectoid-style sheaf-theoretic behavior in relative settings.

thm:5.3.6.summary: The sheaves $\tilde{\mathcal{E}}^{\mathrm{int}}$ and $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}^{\mathrm{int},r}$ satisfy the Kiehl glueing property. Sheafy property of the relative Robba rings — required for $\varphi$-modules to assemble into a sheaf theory.

thm:5.3.9.summary: The Banach rings $\tilde{\mathcal{E}}_R$, $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}^{\mathrm{bd},r}_R$, and $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}^{[s,r]}_R$ are relatively perfectoid; via thm:3.7.4, any extension to an adic Banach ring is stably uniform, sheafy, and has Tate/Kiehl glueing. The bridge that makes Robba-ring constructions live inside the perfectoid world.

thm:5.5.9.summary: Almost purity: for a perfectoid algebra $A$ and any $B \in \mathbf{F\acute{E}t}(A)$, $\mathfrak{o}_B$ is uniformly almost finite projective and almost étale over $\mathfrak{o}_A$. Parallel to Scholze thm:1.10; their main almost-purity result derived through the Kedlaya–Liu framework.

thm:4.1.9.summary: Slope-purity criterion: a $\varphi$-module $M$ over $\mathcal{R}_K$ with $\mu(M) = s$ is pure of slope $s$ iff no nonzero proper $\varphi$-submodule has slope $> s$.

thm:4.1.10.summary: Slope filtration over the Robba ring: any nonzero $\varphi$-module $M$ over $\mathcal{R}_K$ has a unique filtration by saturated $\varphi$-submodules with pure successive quotients of strictly decreasing slopes. The classical Kedlaya slope filtration.

thm:4.2.13.summary: Slope filtration over the extended Robba ring $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}_L$: same uniqueness statement as thm:4.1.10 in the Witt-vector setting. The relative-version-ready form of slope theory.

thm:6.3.9.summary: For $\varphi^a$-bundles over $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}_R$ and $f \in P_d$, $M_f$ is finite projective over $P[f^{-1}]_0$. Comparison-of-localizations input to the FF-curve equivalence.

thm:6.3.12.summary: Equivalence of tensor categories: quasicoherent finite locally free sheaves on $\mathrm{Proj}(P)$ $\cong$ $\varphi^a$-modules over $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}^\infty_R$ $\cong$ $\varphi^a$-modules over $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}_R$ $\cong$ $\varphi^a$-bundles over $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}_R$. The Fargues–Fontaine vector-bundle dictionary in the relative setting.

thm:7.4.5.summary: Lower semicontinuity of the slope polygon: for any $\varphi^a$-module $M$ over $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}_R$, the slope polygon of $M \otimes \tilde{\mathcal{R}}_{\mathcal{H}(\beta)}$ varies lower-semicontinuously in $\beta \in \mathcal{M}(R)$. Geometric variation of slopes.

thm:7.4.9.summary: Slope filtration in families: under appropriate constancy of partial slope sums, a $\varphi^a$-module over $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}_R$ admits a unique submodule realizing the prescribed slope splitting. The relative slope filtration.

thm:8.3.5.summary: Equivalence between perfectoid adic spaces and pairs $(X, \mathcal{I})$ where $X$ is a perfect adic space and $\mathcal{I}$ is an ideal subsheaf locally generated by a primitive-of-degree-$1$ element. Globalizes thm:3.6.5; parallel to Scholze thm:1.9.

thm:8.5.5.summary: For $X$ a perfectoid adic space over $\mathbb{Q}_{p^d}$ and $X'$ its perfect-uniform tilt, étale $\mathbb{Z}_{p^d}$-local systems on $X$, on $X'$, and on suitable adic spaces with Frobenius-perfection $X'$ are equivalent categories. Parallel to Scholze thm:1.11 (étale-site equivalence) at the local-system level.

thm:8.5.6.summary: For $(R,R^+)$ a perfect uniform adic Banach algebra over $\mathbb{F}_{p^d}$, isogeny $\mathbb{Z}_{p^d}$-local systems descend along Frobenius perfection. Lets phi-module data be tracked across Witt-vector liftings.

thm:8.5.8.summary: Isogeny $(c,d)$-$\mathbb{Z}_p$-local systems descend along Frobenius perfection. Refines thm:8.5.6 with twisting parameters needed for slope-filtered phi-module input.

thm:8.6.2.summary: For an étale $\mathbb{Z}_{p^d}$-local system $T$ on $\mathrm{Spec}(R)$ and the corresponding $\varphi^d$-module $M$ over $\tilde{\mathcal{R}}^{\mathrm{int}}_R$ or $W(R)$, there are natural bijections $H^i_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(X, T) \cong H^i_{\varphi^d}(M)$. Cohomology comparison: étale $=$ $\varphi$-module.

thm:9.2.15.summary: On a perfectoid or perfect-uniform $X$, pullback gives an equivalence between finite locally free $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules and finite locally free $\widehat{\mathcal{O}}_X$-modules. Pro-étale completion is harmless on perfectoid spaces.

thm:9.3.7.summary: Equivalence of categories: étale $\mathbb{Z}_{p^d}$-local systems over $X$ $\cong$ $\varphi^d$-modules over $\tilde{\mathbf{A}}_X$ $\cong$ $\varphi^d$-modules over $\tilde{\mathbf{A}}^\dagger_X$. Relative version of the étale-cohomology / phi-module dictionary.

thm:9.3.12.summary: Functorial equivalence: $\varphi^d$-modules over $\tilde{\mathbf{C}}_X$ $\cong$ vector bundles on the relative Fargues–Fontaine curve $\mathrm{FF}_X$ $\cong$ $B$-pairs on $X$. The geometric incarnation of the local-system / phi-module correspondence.

thm:9.4.2.summary: Pro-étale cohomology of an étale local system $E$ matches $\varphi^d$-module cohomology of the associated $M^\dagger$ (on $\tilde{\mathbf{A}}^\dagger_X$) and $M$ (on $\tilde{\mathbf{A}}_X$). Generalizes thm:8.6.2 to pro-étale topology.

thm:9.5.6.summary: For $K/\mathbb{Q}_p$ finite, continuous representations of $G_K$ on finite-dimensional $\mathbb{Q}_p$-vector spaces are canonically equivalent to étale $(\varphi, \Gamma)$-modules over $\mathbf{B}_K$, $\mathbf{B}^\dagger_K$, $\mathbf{C}_K$, and $\tilde{\mathbf{C}}_K$. Recovers Fontaine's classical $(\varphi, \Gamma)$-module theory inside the relative-perfectoid framework — the headline application.

thm:9.5.8.summary: For $K/\mathbb{Q}_p$ finite and $F = \widehat{\mathbb{Q}_p(\mu_{p^\infty})}$, $(\varphi, \Gamma)$-modules over $\mathbf{C}_K$ and over $\tilde{\mathbf{C}}_K$ are equivalent to $B$-pairs over $K$ and to continuous $\Gamma$-equivariant vector bundles on $\mathrm{FF}_{\mathrm{Spa}(F \otimes K, \dots)}$. Geometric reformulation linking $(\varphi, \Gamma)$-modules to Fargues–Fontaine bundles.

edges.note: Galois equivalence is the finite-extension version of the perfectoid correspondence at field level.
Banach-algebra correspondence specializes to the field correspondence on residue fields.
Field-level Galois equivalence supplies the residue-field input.
Spa-level homeomorphism lifts the Gel'fand-spectrum identification implicit in the algebra correspondence.
Sheafy / stably uniform inherited from the perfectoid structure provided by the correspondence.
Spa-level identification needed for sheaf statements.
Finite étale tensor equivalence is the finite-étale lift of the algebra-level perfectoid correspondence.
Density of bounded homomorphisms used to transfer perfectoid structure to finite étale extensions.
Deeply ramified hypothesis powers the Faltings-style almost étale lifting.
Preperfectoid stability uses the perfectoid correspondence on each rational localization.
Sheafy property inherited via the stably-uniform conclusion.
Relative-Robba sheaf properties follow once these rings are exhibited as relatively perfectoid.
Kiehl glueing follows from the sheafy / stably-uniform consequence of perfectoid structure.
Almost purity reduces to the finite-étale tensor equivalence via the perfectoid correspondence.
Deep ramification supplies the input to the Gabber–Ramero almost-étale framework.
Slope filtration uniqueness uses the slope-purity criterion as inductive engine.
Witt-vector / extended Robba slope filtration parallels the classical Robba-ring statement.
Relative perfectoid structure on Robba rings underpins the localization arguments.
Localization-finite-projective input lifts the FF-curve / phi-module dictionary.
Pointwise slope filtration is the input whose semicontinuity is being analyzed.
Slope filtration in families uses the semicontinuity to glue pointwise filtrations.
Pointwise extended-Robba slope filtration is the local input.
Sheafification of the algebra-level perfectoid correspondence.
Spa-level homeomorphism gives the topological underpinning for the sheaf-of-ideals formulation.
Local-system equivalence reduces to the étale topos of the corresponding perfect adic space.
Almost purity ensures finite étale covers stay perfectoid, making site equivalence well-defined.
Isogeny version on perfect uniform adic Banach algebras.
Adds $(c,d)$-twisted refinements for slope-filtered phi-modules.
Cohomology comparison follows once local-system categories are identified.
Phi-module / vector-bundle dictionary supplies the cohomological model.
Pro-étale completion harmlessness is set up on the perfectoid space side.
Pro-étale local systems pull back from the perfectoid étale level.
Completion-vs-uncompleted equivalence lets pro-étale local systems inherit phi-module structure.
Geometric incarnation: phi-modules over $\tilde{\mathbf{C}}_X$ become vector bundles on the relative FF curve via the $\mathrm{Proj}(P)$ dictionary.
Pro-étale cohomology comparison follows from the local-system / phi-module dictionary.
Generalizes the étale-cohomology equality to pro-étale topology.
Relative-($\varphi,\Gamma$)-module recovery of Fontaine's theory specializes the relative local-system / phi-module dictionary.
Cohomological comparison is what makes the equivalence canonical.
B-pair / FF-bundle equivalence specializes the relative geometric incarnation.
Drops the étale-condition: extends classical $(\varphi,\Gamma)$-module theory to all (not necessarily étale) $(\varphi,\Gamma)$-modules.

perfectoid_correspondence.description: Sections 3.5–3.7. Kedlaya–Liu's Witt-vector formulation of the perfectoid correspondence: equivalence between perfectoid analytic objects and pairs (perfect characteristic-$p$ object, primitive-degree-$1$ ideal in $W(\mathfrak{o}_\bullet)$). Parallel to Scholze §3–§5 and §6.

almost_purity.description: Section 5.5. Almost-purity statement; parallel to Scholze thm:1.10 obtained inside the Kedlaya–Liu uniform-Banach framework.

slope_theory.description: Section 4. Kedlaya's slope filtrations over the Robba ring and its Witt-vector extension. The Kedlaya–Liu-specific machinery the relative theory transports.

relative_robba_and_FF_curve.description: Sections 5.3 and 6.3. Relative Robba rings shown to be relatively perfectoid (giving sheafy/Kiehl-glueing properties); $\varphi$-modules identified with vector bundles on the relative Fargues–Fontaine curve.

perfectoid_spaces_and_local_systems.description: Section 8. Perfectoid adic spaces (sheaf-of-ideals incarnation); étale and isogeny local systems descend through the perfectoid–perfect correspondence; cohomology equates étale to $\varphi$-module.

relative_phi_gamma_modules.description: Section 9. The headline application: relative ($\varphi, \Gamma$)-modules. Recovers and extends Fontaine's classical theory inside the perfectoid framework, with a geometric incarnation via Fargues–Fontaine bundles.

Scholze:Perfectoid.title: Perfectoid spaces
Scholze:Perfectoid.role: Parallel development of the perfectoid framework. Section 7 of this paper is dedicated to comparing the two frameworks.

FaltingsAlmostEtale.title: Almost étale extensions
FaltingsAlmostEtale.role: Almost mathematics; precursor of almost purity.

GabberRamero.title: Almost ring theory
GabberRamero.role: Systematic almost ring theory. Used at thm:3.5.11 and almost-purity proofs.

Huber:Adic.title: Étale Cohomology of Rigid Analytic Varieties and Adic Spaces

Berkovich:SpectralTheory.title: Spectral theory and analytic geometry over non-Archimedean fields
Berkovich:SpectralTheory.role: Non-archimedean analytic geometry; the Gel'fand spectrum framework used throughout §2–§3.

Fontaine:PhiGamma.title: Représentations p-adiques des corps locaux
Fontaine:PhiGamma.role: Classical $(\varphi, \Gamma)$-module theory for $G_K$-representations. Recovered in thm:9.5.6.

Kedlaya:SlopeFiltration.title: Slope filtrations revisited
Kedlaya:SlopeFiltration.role: Original Kedlaya slope filtration over the Robba ring; thm:4.1.10 here.

FarguesFontaine:Curve.title: Courbes et fibrés vectoriels en théorie de Hodge p-adique
FarguesFontaine:Curve.role: Fargues–Fontaine curve; vector-bundle perspective used in §6.3 and §9.3.
